title: Office of Space Commerce makes its case for mission authorization
summary: The Office of Space Commerce advocated for a mission authorization framework to offer regulatory clarity for companies while minimizing bureaucratic hurdles. The proposal aims to address novel space activities under a streamlined regulatory approach.

title: Bluestaq Launches BLUESTAQ / ARQ Defense-Grade Data Infrastructure Now Available for Commercial, Government, and Enterprise Organizations
summary: Bluestaq launched BLUESTAQ / ARQ, a defense-grade data infrastructure platform for commercial, government, and enterprise organizations. The platform is designed for sectors including healthcare and other industries.

title: Swissto12 raises $70 million to accelerate small GEO satellite production
summary: Swissto12 has secured $70 million in funding to boost its production of small geostationary (GEO) satellites. The investment aims to meet increasing demand in the satellite manufacturing sector.

title: Space Force awards Slingshot $69 million for AI-enabled training technology
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Slingshot a $69 million contract for AI-enabled training technology to simulate satellite-defense scenarios over 4.5 years. This initiative aims to enhance space domain awareness and defensive capabilities.

title: Saltzman’s farewell warning: Prepare for war in space to preserve peace
summary: Gen. Chance Saltzman, departing Space Force chief, warned of potential war in space and emphasized the need for combat readiness and stronger alliances. He also highlighted the limitations of procurement reform in his final public speech.

title: Commercial Space Federation Launches State and Local Council to Align States on National Space Capacity and Strengthen the U.S. Industrial Base
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) launched a State and Local Council to align U.S. states on national space capacity and bolster the industrial base. This initiative aims to strengthen the U.S. space industry's competitiveness and coordination.

title: Startup working with University of Texas focuses on trapping small debris
summary: Florida startup SOAR partners with the University of Texas, El Paso (UTEP) to develop a passive system for trapping small space debris. The collaboration aims to address the growing issue of orbital debris.

title: Icarus Robotics taps KULR to provide batteries for Space Station robots
summary: Icarus Robotics, a New York-based startup, has chosen KULR Technology Group to supply batteries for Joy, a free-flying robot platform intended for space missions. The collaboration aims to support robotic operations on the Space Station.

title: Laying the groundwork for the first human mission to Mars
summary: The article discusses the complexities and preparations involved in planning the first human mission to Mars. It highlights the monumental challenges of safely sending humans to the Red Planet and returning them to Earth.

title: Satellite investment sets annual record halfway through 2026
summary: Satellite investment reached $8.1 billion in the first half of 2026, already surpassing the total annual investment tracked by early-stage investor Space Capital.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 21 'data transport' satellites for US military today
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 21 satellites for the U.S. military's Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation today. The event can be watched live.

title: Is dark matter 'tuned in' to a hidden dimension?
summary: New research suggests a potential link between the mysteries of dark matter and the existence of higher dimensions. The study proposes that dark matter may be 'tuned in' to a hidden dimension.

title: SpaceX will launch Starship, the world's largest rocket, on critical Flight 13 test today. Here's what to expect.
summary: SpaceX is set to launch the 13th test flight of Starship, the world's largest rocket, today. The mission will deploy 'V3' Starlink satellites for the first time.

title: 'Full steam ahead': NASA ramps up prep for Artemis III astronaut launch in 2027
summary: NASA is accelerating preparations for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch with crewed lunar ambitions. The agency is assembling hardware and conducting launch simulations in mission control.

title: Watch Archimedes burn! Rocket Lab fires up engine for its powerful next-gen Neutron launcher (video)
summary: Rocket Lab conducted a full-duration static fire test of the engine for the second stage of its Neutron launch vehicle. The Neutron is a next-generation rocket designed to be more powerful and capable.

title: Meteorite that crashed through New Jersey house could hold the clues to life's origins
summary: A meteorite that crashed through a New Jersey house may provide insights into the origins of life. The pristine condition of the CM1/2 meteorite is attributed to the homeowner's quick reaction.

title: Raspberry sugar spotted in interstellar space, a sweet discovery that could reveal clues about life's origins
summary: Scientists have detected raspberry sugar (methyl formate) in interstellar space, a discovery that may provide insights into the origins of life. This finding supports the idea that complex organic molecules can form naturally in space.

title: The 10 best places in the UK to see the partial solar eclipse on Aug. 12
summary: The UK will experience its deepest partial solar eclipse in decades on August 12, coinciding with the peak of the Perseid meteor shower. The news highlights the best locations in the UK to view the eclipse.

title: Glaciers spotted flowing across the Himalayas from space | Space photo of the day for July 15, 2026
summary: Satellite imagery captured glaciers flowing across the Himalayas, showcasing their movement downhill from space. This highlights the dynamic nature of glacial landscapes in the region.

title: Starship Flight 13: Potentially the final Suborbital Flight
summary: Starship Flight 13 is potentially the final suborbital flight for the Starship program, following the first Block 3 Starship flight with Booster 19 two months prior.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another crewed mission to the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th test flight. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. The telescope is undergoing preparations for its upcoming mission.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20 with 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant infrastructure development with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's ongoing revitalization as a major hub for space launches.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights are also expected.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a commercial destination in low Earth orbit.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, informing industry about planned activities and opportunities across ESA programmes.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to perform multiple tasks. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these researchers.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns over the Office of Space Commerce's ability to fund and implement the framework.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has secured $70 million in Series C funding to expand its production of sovereign hardware for orbital use. The investment will help the company meet growing global demand for in-space infrastructure.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand the company's offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned the nominees on space policy during the hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, increasing the total number of companies in the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe will contribute thermal management solutions for ESA's Moonraker mission, which is planned for 2030. The collaboration aims to address the challenges of extreme temperature variations in lunar orbit.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall. The company announced the milestone today.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns are beyond its regulatory scope.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their mission of enabling in-space power transfer.

title: FCC to Consider Allowing Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Devices for D2D Service
summary: The FCC is considering allowing unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites for direct-to-device (D2D) service. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr proposed a new proceeding to explore this possibility.

title: South Africa’s Top ISP Herotel to Distribute Amazon Leo
summary: South Africa's leading ISP Herotel will distribute Amazon's Leo satellite connectivity to residential customers. The deal marks Herotel's expansion into satellite-based internet distribution.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense COO Kevin Bidlack Talks Growth Post-IPO in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense's COO Kevin Bidlack discusses the company's growth following its IPO in a recent interview. The company, which recently went public, is highlighted for its diverse space tech offerings.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Wins Space Force Contract to Expand AI Mission Rehearsal
summary: Slingshot Aerospace secured a nearly $70 million contract with the U.S. Space Force, marking the largest deal in its history. The contract aims to expand AI-driven mission rehearsal capabilities.

title: SDA Set to Resume Launches With Second Plane of York Transport Layer Satellites
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is set to resume launches with the second plane of 21 York Space Systems data transport satellites after a nine-month pause. The launch marks a significant step in the York Transport Layer satellite program.

title: Honeywell to Lead ESA Quantum Space Magnetometer Contract
summary: Honeywell Aerospace will lead a consortium to develop a compact quantum space magnetometer for studying Earth's magnetic field, partnering with Quantum Brilliance and Jagiellonian University. The project is funded by the European Space Agency (ESA).

title: NRL Awards Parsons $245M Satellite Ground Systems Contract for Tracking Facility
summary: The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory awarded Parsons Corporation's subsidiary a $245 million contract to support satellite ground systems software and operations at a tracking facility. This contract extends the company's ongoing work in satellite ground infrastructure.

title: Frontier to Adopt Starlink With New Customer Portal From SpaceX
summary: Frontier Airlines will adopt SpaceX's Starlink in-flight connectivity for over 1,000 aircraft under a new deal. The agreement includes a new customer portal from SpaceX.

title: Iridium’s PNT Chip is Now Commercially Available
summary: Iridium has made its positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) chip commercially available, with over 150 organizations showing interest. The chip was first announced in October.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris Technologies and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the SDA Tranche 3 Tracking Layer satellites, with L3Harris receiving $955 million and Sierra Space $798 million under rapid prototyping agreements.
